detail: 12mm diameter Edison screw. Standard for US candelabra fixtures, chandeliers, and decorative sconces.
14mm diameter Edison screw. Standard for EU candelabra and small decorative fixtures. Runs at 240V.
26mm diameter Edison screw. The standard US household base for table lamps, floor lamps, and most ceiling fixtures.
27mm diameter Edison screw. The standard EU/UK household base. Nearly the same size as E26 but not certified for mixing.
Two straight 4mm-spaced pins. Used for 12V low-voltage bulbs in landscape, RV, and marine lighting.
Two straight 5.3mm-spaced pins with thicker wires. Used for 12V MR16 spotlights in track and display lighting.
Two looped/folded pins in a capsule form. Mains-voltage (120–240V) base common in European-style wall sconces.
Two mushroom-tipped pins 10mm apart that lock with a quarter-turn. Mains voltage. Common in recessed downlights and track lighting.
Larger twist-lock base with 24mm spacing, designed for CFL/LED energy-code compliance in US recessed lighting.
22mm push-and-turn bayonet base with two side pins. Standard in UK, Australia, and parts of Asia. Does not screw in.
